temporal_candidate_diagnostics.note: Diagnostic temporal-candidate coverage only; benchmark-facing metrics use gan_frequency_deterministic_v1 on model predictions.

candidates.derivation: two explicitly dated events define the observation window
candidates.evidence_text: Seizure history and current status: His initial event was in April 2019 in Germany, arising from sleep. He awoke with jerking of the left arm and facial twitching. A second event occurred in Italy the following July 2019, once more during the night, lasting four minutes with similar semiology.

candidate_labels: multiple cluster per week, 2 to 3 per cluster

candidates.canonical_label: multiple cluster per week, 2 to 3 per cluster
candidates.derivation: several mornings each week with intra-morning repeats; per-cluster range preserved without inventing cluster spacing
candidates.evidence_text: Over the past six weeks he has noticed that brief episodes are tending to bunch together after he wakes, occurring on several mornings each week despite taking his tablets as prescribed. He describes these as short spells of disorientation with a metallic taste and a need to pause for 30–60 seconds, without loss of awareness, sometimes repeating two or three times within the same morning.

candidates.evidence_text: In terms of seizure control, She had no seizures for nearly a year following initiation of Valproate, then developed myoclonic jerks leading to a tonic seizure three Saturdays ago

candidates.evidence_text: Regarding frequency, the patient reports that episodes tend to come in small runs: typically around four to six short spells grouped together on days when they occur, most commonly later in the day towards the evening period.

She has remained seizure-free since then

candidates.derivation: named-month event diary summed through clinic date
candidates.evidence_text: Regarding recent events: In February she experienced a prolonged focal seizure lasting 8 minutes, resolving without intervention. In May there were four further brief absences, and in August a single generalised tonic-clonic seizure was reported at school (not witnessed by family). These episodes have been clearly recorded in her diary, with triggers likely related to missed meals and sleep deprivation around coursework deadlines. Since adopting structured reminders in late August, she has had no further events and has taken all doses on time
